name: Addis Invoice OCR
description: AI-powered invoice processing pipeline for small businesses in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. Users submit invoices (PDF/JPG/PNG) via a React web dashboard, Telegram bot, or WhatsApp. n8n orchestrates Google Document AI for OCR text extraction and DeepSeek for structured data parsing. Extracted data (vendor, date, line items, grand total) is written to Google Sheets and surfaced in a searchable, filterable invoice history with detail modals and PDF export.

frontend.name: addis-invoice-dashboard

pages./: Landing page with hero, feature highlights, how-it-works steps, and CTA sections styled in cyan theme
pages./upload: Drag-and-drop invoice upload with progress bar, processing spinner, and result card with detail modal
pages./invoices: Searchable, filterable, sortable invoice history table with detail modal and PDF download

components: Navbar — top navigation with app branding and page links
UploadZone — drag-and-drop file upload area with type/size validation
ProcessingSpinner — animated spinner shown during OCR+AI extraction
ResultsCard — extracted invoice preview with line items table and action buttons (Save, Download, View Invoice, Upload Another)
FilterBar — search, date range, and clear filters for invoice list
InvoiceTable — paginated, sortable table with view, download, and delete actions
DetailsModal — full-screen overlay showing vendor, date, total, source, line items, items summary, and PDF export

hooks.useInvoiceUpload: Manages upload lifecycle — idle → uploading → processing → uploaded/completed/failed. Posts multipart FormData to n8n webhook via centralized API client. Handles progress, timeout (150s), CORS, and React StrictMode remount safety.
hooks.useInvoiceList: Fetches invoice history from n8n get-invoices webhook. Provides search, date filtering, sorting, pagination, and delete (local hide).

lib.api: Centralized Axios instance pointing to VITE_API_URL. Appends webhook paths automatically. Supports dev/prod environment switching via .env files.

OCR-3.3-web.description: Main invoice processing workflow with three entry points: Web upload, Telegram bot, and WhatsApp.

flow: 2. Generate Job ID — creates job_id, status=queued, passes binary file
3. Respond to Webhook — immediately returns {"status":"success","message":"..."} with CORS headers
4. Extract from File — Google Document AI OCR text extraction
5. Switch PDF/Image — routes to appropriate HTTP Request node
6. AI Agent + DeepSeek Chat Model + Structured Output Parser — parses vendor, date, items, grand_total from extracted text
7. Edit Fields — normalizes and formats extracted data
8. If valid invoice → Append or update row in Google Sheets
9. If not valid → Send rejection message (Telegram only)
10. Switch → Send Telegram/WhatsApp notification on success
1. Telegram Trigger — listens for file messages
2. Get a file — downloads file from Telegram
3. Extract from File — Google Document AI OCR
4. Switch PDF/Image → HTTP Request to Document AI
5. Converges with web path at AI Agent for parsing and Sheets storage

whatsapp_path.flow: WhatsApp integration added in v3.3 — receives invoice files via WhatsApp and feeds into the shared OCR+AI pipeline

ai_models.ocr: Google Document AI (australia-southeast1 region, processor for PDF/image OCR)
ai_models.parsing: DeepSeek Chat Model — deepseek-v4-flash (via n8n LangChain nodes with Structured Output Parser)

structured_output_schema.is_invoice: boolean — true if valid invoice/receipt, false for unrelated content
structured_output_schema.date: string — invoice date (empty if not an invoice)
structured_output_schema.vendor_name: string — standardized vendor name, Amharic translated to English (empty if not an invoice)
structured_output_schema.grand_total: number — final total amount due (0 if not an invoice)
structured_output_schema.items_summary: string — concise text summary of purchased items (empty if not an invoice)

immediate_ack.message: File uploaded successfully! Your invoice is being processed in the background.

response_format.async_result: Written to Google Sheets, surfaced via get-invoices webhook

get-invoices.description: GET webhook that reads all rows from Google Sheets, takes the 15 most recent, reverses them (newest first), and returns as a JSON array with normalized field names.
flow: 3. Code node — slices last 15 rows, reverses, normalizes fields (vendor, date, grand_total, items_summary, source)

backend.data_store: Google Sheets — append/update rows via n8n Google Sheets node (Sheet: OCR-dev → Sheet1)

data_flow.upload: User submits file via Web/Telegram/WhatsApp → n8n acknowledges immediately → Background: Document AI OCR → DeepSeek AI parsing → Google Sheets → Telegram/WhatsApp notification
data_flow.history: Frontend GET /get-invoices → n8n reads Google Sheets → Returns last 15 rows (newest first) → Frontend displays in sortable/filterable table
data_flow.detail_view: User clicks View Invoice → Frontend opens DetailsModal overlay with full invoice data and PDF download

channels.web: React dashboard with drag-and-drop upload at /upload
channels.telegram: Telegram bot accepting invoice files and returning extracted summary
channels.whatsapp: WhatsApp channel for invoice submission (added in v3.3)

key_fixes_applied: Removed manual Content-Type: multipart/form-data header — let axios set boundary automatically
Fixed React StrictMode double-mount bug — mountedRef not reset to true on remount, silently dropping all upload state callbacks
Added View Invoice button to upload result screen — fetches latest invoice from API and opens DetailsModal
Centralized API configuration in lib/api.ts with VITE_API_URL env var and localhost fallback
Separated dev/prod environments with single VITE_API_URL in .env files
Redesigned homepage with landing page sections (hero, features, how-it-works, CTA) and cyan color theme
Rebranded all Ledger references to Addis Invoice
target_users: Small businesses in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. Supports Amharic and English invoice formats.

deployment.frontend: Vercel (build: npm run build, output: dist/)
